focus: Quantify full-tree missing-root-spec scope across 026, determine whether save_lineage is ever persisted, isolate the workflow-to-graph writeback break, and draft validator assertions with migration paths.

graphEvents.label: Full 026 tree scan outside research/review finds four packet roots with description.json and graph-metadata.json but no root spec.md: 007, 008, 009, and 010
All four missing-root-spec packet roots also serialize graph source_docs as an empty array, confirming no canonical doc surface is available to graph metadata
All 96 packet graph-metadata files outside research/review serialize derived.save_lineage as null or missing; none stores a non-null lineage
workflow.ts passes saveLineage same_pass, but mcp_server/api/indexing.ts exports refreshGraphMetadata(specFolder) and drops the options object before graph refresh
Live packet identity drift persists in 013 and 015 across continuity packet_pointer and description.specFolder normalization
012 still shows description.lastUpdated materially newer than graph last_save_at, indicating a partial follow-up asymmetry class remains live
Validator rule root-spec-required-when-metadata-exists should fail packet roots that carry description.json or graph-metadata.json without spec.md
Validator rule graph-source-docs-required-for-active-packet should fail packet roots whose graph metadata has an empty source_docs array
Validator rule save-lineage-required-on-fresh-graph-save should fail fresh graph metadata writes that omit save_lineage after the wrapper fix cutoff
Validator rule packet-identity-normalization should warn or fail when description, continuity, and graph packet identities do not normalize to the same packet-relative path
Validator rule description-newer-than-graph-on-recent-packet should flag recent packets whose description freshness materially exceeds graph freshness
